children.name: title
children.text: How each OOD-severity table cell is computed

children.name: subtitle
children.text: Cell entry = test-set size; Wasserstein shift in property space / representation space
Cell format

children.name: cell-format-formula
children.alt: n test; W1(y) divided by W1(z)

children.text: Read as: number of OOD test samples; target-property distribution shift divided by 2D material-representation shift.
nₜₑₛₜ
Counts how many samples are held out under the OOD split protocol.
Symbol: Dₜₑₛₜ = held-out OOD test set.
W₁(y)
Measures how far the test target-property distribution is from the training target-property distribution.
Physical meaning: property extrapolation severity, e.g., UTS or El shift.
W₁(z)
Measures how far the test sample cloud is from the training cloud in 2D t-SNE representation space.
Physical meaning: material-feature / chemistry-process space shift.
Wasserstein-1 distance: minimum transport cost
Intuition: the average distance needed to ‘move’ one empirical distribution into another.
Empirical distributions:
LOCO aggregation
When multiple groups are held out:
same weighted average for W₁(z)
k indexes the held-out group/fold; larger held-out groups contribute more.
Interpretation: larger W₁(y) = stronger property shift; larger W₁(z) = stronger material-representation shift.
